name: morph-calendar-event
description: A calendar day cell that morphs into an event-detail panel: the date tile is a single persistent element that travels and scales from the collapsed cell into the card header — a real shared-element move, not a crossfade — as the Card springs in around it. The event title + kind Badge, a time and location row with tinted icon tiles, an Avatar stack of attendees, a Separator-split note and an Accept/Maybe Button pair stagger in on open and reverse-stagger out on close. Built on framer-motion via block-motion, so the whole morph collapses to a plain crossfade under prefers-reduced-motion.

notes: The collapsed day cell is a single labelled toggle ("View event: …" + aria-expanded); when open the persistent tile becomes decorative (disabled, aria-hidden, out of the tab order) and the card's labelled close control takes over
The event title renders as plain text (not a heading) so it never forces a heading level onto the host page; the attendees are wrapped in a group labelled with the going count
The attendee avatars and every icon/dot are decorative and aria-hidden; the going count carries the meaning in text
The travel/scale + stagger morph respects prefers-reduced-motion via MotionProvider, collapsing to a plain crossfade

intents: a calendar day cell that expands into an event-detail panel
a shared-element morph from a date tile to an event card
an animated event popover built on ibirdui
